message:
feat(security): implement clipboard policy API, link audit trail, and wide character handling

- Add ClipboardPolicy struct with copy_enabled, paste_enabled, and
  max_paste_bytes fields plus host-configurable setter/getter APIs
- Add clipboardPolicy and setClipboardPolicy to public JS method surface
- Enforce clipboard policy in copy_selection() and paste_text() with
  custom byte limits
- Add ResolvedLinkClick audit fields: policy_rule, action_outcome,
  audit_url (scheme+host only), audit_url_redacted flag
- Add redact_url_for_audit() to extract scheme and host without
  sensitive path components
- Implement trim_trailing_spaces() and infer_wide_continuations() for
  correct CJK/wide glyph handling in selection extraction
- Update frame_harness LinkClickSnapshot with audit trail fields and
  serialization
- Add 15+ new unit tests for clipboard policy enforcement, URL
  redaction, and wide character selection
- Update threat model and web API specs with clipboard policy defaults
  and audit field documentation
- Close bd-2vr05.11.2 (clipboard/link hardening), advance bd-2vr05.4

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.10.5","title":"task(ci): wire conformance/differential/fuzz checks into release-blocking gates","description":"Make advanced quality suites mandatory release gates with clear triage instructions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior in this task is deterministic for fixed inputs and capability profile.
- Required unit and/or E2E tests are added for the scoped behavior.
- Scope is complete only when dependencies remain satisfied and no regressions are introduced.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This task must add or update focused unit tests for core logic touched.
- This task must link to at least one end-to-end script path that validates integrated behavior.
- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Comprehensive unit tests are implemented for the scoped subsystem, including edge cases and regression vectors.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are implemented for the scoped workflows and runnable in CI.
- Test execution emits detailed structured logs (JSONL) including timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context.
- At least one deliberate failure injection scenario is covered and produces actionable diagnostics.","notes":"Execution Notes
- Conformance should directly track declared support-matrix claims.
- Differential harness should normalize expected differences and classify mismatches clearly.
- Fuzzing must produce minimized repro artifacts suitable for deterministic regression tests.
- CI gates from this track are intended to be release-blocking, not advisory.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:56.818319100Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:35.669742782Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","frankenterm","gates","quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:56.818319100Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:35.513950227Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.10.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:35.881786564Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:36.255031604Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:36.640719835Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:35.669683851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.11","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): security posture, bounded resources, and operational observability","description":"Background
- Remote terminal mode introduces meaningful security and reliability risks.
- Production operations need deterministic traces and bounded-resource behavior.

Goal
- Define secure defaults and observability foundations for safe remote deployment.

In Scope
- Threat model for attach/embedding architecture.
- Link/clipboard policy hardening.
- Queue and memory cap enforcement.
- Deterministic trace and replay capabilities.
- Operational runbooks for incident handling.

Out of Scope
- Security assumptions left implicit.

Why This Matters
- Essential for production safety and maintainability.

Acceptance Criteria
- Threat model maps to concrete implementation tasks.
- Overload behavior is bounded and test-backed.
- Incident replay and runbooks support effective triage.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Feature behavior is validated by comprehensive unit tests that cover core invariants and edge cases.
- Feature behavior is validated by end-to-end test scripts that exercise realistic user workflows.
- E2E runs emit detailed structured logs (JSONL or equivalent) sufficient for deterministic failure triage and replay.
- CI gates for this feature fail on regression in correctness, determinism, or declared standards scope.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:37.820444865Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:15.420385236Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","ops","reliability","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:37.820444865Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:08.145118822Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.11.1","title":"task(security): author threat model for remote attach and embedding","description":"Trust boundaries, attack scenarios, and mitigation mapping for remote/browser architecture.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior in this task is deterministic for fixed inputs and capability profile.
- Required unit and/or E2E tests are added for the scoped behavior.
- Scope is complete only when dependencies remain satisfied and no regressions are introduced.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This task must add or update focused unit tests for core logic touched.
- This task must link to at least one end-to-end script path that validates integrated behavior.
- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Threat model maps to concrete mitigations and open risks.
- Policy defaults for links/clipboard are safe and explicit.
- Queue/memory caps are enforced under adversarial load.
- Trace/replay and runbooks support incident triage workflows.

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","notes":"Execution Notes
- Security defaults should assume hostile or malformed remote traffic.
- Policy controls (links, clipboard) must have safe defaults and explicit override points.
- Resource caps should be hard limits with predictable overload behavior.
- Trace/replay outputs should support post-incident root-cause workflows.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:57.196609809Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-15T01:30:16.804117362Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T01:30:16.804098888Z","close_reason":"Completed: threat model authored, secure defaults enforced, tests added","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","security","threat-model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.11.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:57.196609809Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Viewport and scrollback mappings are deterministic under navigation and resize.
- Follow-output behavior matches documented policy.
- Search across history is stable under continuous output.
- Performance/memory tests satisfy defined budgets.

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","notes":"Execution Notes
- Viewport semantics should remain stable under continuous output and user scrolling.
- Follow-output state transitions must be explicit (user override versus automatic resume).
- Resize behavior must be policy-driven and documented (especially reflow or no-reflow decisions).
- Performance tests should include pathological histories, not just happy-path demos.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:41.252603119Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:14:57.965708999Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:14:57.965686918Z","close_reason":"Completed resize-safe viewport semantics and explicit follow-output policy in frankenterm-web wasm/scroll with deterministic tests; validated by cargo fmt/check/clippy/test and wasm32 no-run; quick E2E emitted structured logs with 13/14 pass and pre-existing inline_basic assertion failure","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","resize","scrollback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:41.252603119Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:12.721414144Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Performance tests should include pathological histories, not just happy-path demos.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:41.991212688Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:22:29.140336922Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:22:29.140317305Z","close_reason":"Completed large-scrollback stress, bounded render-window, and failure-injection tests in frankenterm-web wasm; validated by targeted fmt/check/clippy/test and wasm32 no-run compile; quick E2E produced structured logs with 13/14 pass and existing inline_basic assertion failure","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","frankenterm","scrollback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:41.991212688Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:13.498100854Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:13.884277995Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:14.263401903Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2vr05.4","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): selection, copy, and clipboard parity","description":"Background
- Selection and clipboard behavior are non-negotiable for terminal workflows (copy logs, commands, diagnostics).
- Edge cases around wraps, unicode, and scrolling are where many terminal emulators regress.

Goal
- Deliver resilient selection/copy/clipboard behavior with explicit policy and strong test coverage.

In Scope
- Rust-first selection state machine.
- Mouse and keyboard selection gestures.
- Extraction semantics for wrapped and unicode-rich content.
- Clipboard integration and paste-safety interactions.
- Stress testing under churn (output, scroll, resize).

Out of Scope
- Browser-specific one-off hacks that bypass core state model.

Why This Matters
- Reliable selection is a key adoption blocker for replacing xterm.js.

Acceptance Criteria
- Selection remains correct under heavy output and viewport changes.
- Copy output matches documented policy for wraps and graphemes.
- Clipboard flows are deterministic and test-backed.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Feature behavior is validated by comprehensive unit tests that cover core invariants and edge cases.
- Feature behavior is validated by end-to-end test scripts that exercise realistic user workflows.
- E2E runs emit detailed structured logs (JSONL or equivalent) sufficient for deterministic failure triage and replay.
- CI gates for this feature fail on regression in correctness, determinism, or declared standards scope.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:34.723118448Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:15.418193173Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clipboard","frankenterm","selection","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:34.723118448Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:03.985157787Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.4.1","title":"task(selection): build rust-first selection state machine","description":"Linear/rectangular selection model with deterministic range normalization.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior in this task is deterministic for fixed inputs and capability profile.
- Required unit and/or E2E tests are added for the scoped behavior.
- Scope is complete only when dependencies remain satisfied and no regressions are introduced.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This task must add or update focused unit tests for core logic touched.
- This task must link to at least one end-to-end script path that validates integrated behavior.
- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Selection behavior is correct for drag, keyboard extension, and viewport offsets.
- Extraction output matches policy for wraps and unicode graphemes.
- Clipboard integration paths are deterministic and test-covered.
- Stress tests under output churn pass without corruption.

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","notes":"Execution Notes
- Selection logic should live in deterministic core state, not browser-only side effects.
- Ensure extraction rules are documented and fixture-backed for wraps, unicode, and trailing-space policy.
- Clipboard integration must have clear host callback boundaries and explicit fallback behavior.
- Stress cases must include simultaneous output churn and viewport movement.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:42.370104573Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-14T22:53:09.679495093Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T22:53:09.679475296Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","selection","state-machine"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:14.646859293Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:42.370104573Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -55,19 +55,28 @@ Validation:
 
 ### 4.2 Clipboard Policy Defaults
 
+- `clipboardPolicy()` defaults:
+  - `copyEnabled = true`
+  - `pasteEnabled = true`
+  - `maxPasteBytes = 786432`
+  - `hostManagedClipboard = true`
 - No direct clipboard write side effect inside WASM:
   `copySelection()` returns text; host performs clipboard writes.
 - Paste requires explicit host call (`pasteText`) and enforces max payload:
   `MAX_PASTE_BYTES = 786432` bytes decoded UTF-8.
+- Hosts may further tighten policy using `setClipboardPolicy(...)`.
 
 Implementation:
 
-- `crates/frankenterm-web/src/wasm.rs` (`copySelection`, `pasteText`)
+- `crates/frankenterm-web/src/wasm.rs` (`clipboardPolicy`, `setClipboardPolicy`, `copySelection`, `pasteText`)
 - `docs/spec/frankenterm-websocket-protocol.md` (`Clipboard` message size limits)
 
 Validation:
 
+- `clipboard_policy_snapshot_exposes_secure_defaults`
+- `set_clipboard_policy_can_disable_copy_and_paste`
 - `paste_text_rejects_payload_above_max_bytes`
+- `paste_text_respects_clipboard_policy_max_bytes_override`
 - `extract_and_copy_selection_insert_row_breaks_at_grid_boundaries`
 
 ## 5. Bounded-Resource Contract

+    - `pasteEnabled=true`
